summary: Postinstall hook downloads and reflectively executes an XOR-encrypted Windows PE payload
details: On `npm install`, the package's postinstall script (install-hook.js) fetches an opaque binary from https://github.com/Dimitrijenco/Sticky_note/releases/download/v6/payload.bin — a personal GitHub repository unrelated to the package's stated publisher or purpose. The downloaded bytes are XOR-decrypted with key 0x42, allocated into RWX memory via kernel32.VirtualAlloc, copied in with RtlMoveMemory, re-protected to PAGE_EXECUTE_READ, parsed for a PE entry point, and executed in a new thread inside the running node.exe process via kernel32.CreateThread. The payload is never written to disk (in-memory reflective load), and errors are silently swallowed (source comments explicitly say `// Silent fail – do not alert the user.` and `// Reflective Load – No Disk Write`). The package's advertised purpose is a JavaScript chart-statistics library (mean/stddev/trend in index.js); the ffi-napi / ref-napi dependencies and the Win32 loader exist solely to enable in-process execution of attacker-controlled native code. Any Windows developer or CI runner that installs this package executes arbitrary attacker code with the user's privileges.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
